$comment: Workstream H-D: declarative conformance scenario driven by tests/conformance.rs.
Each step is one wire interaction against the residential mock engine. The runner
spawns norves_mock_engine (env NORVES_MOCK_ENGINE), connects the real WS transport,
spawns the Dispatcher, then executes the steps in order.
Schema (one object per step):
kind             'request' | 'event'                                 (required)
method           method name, e.g. 'bridge.hello'                    (required when kind=request)
event            event name, e.g. 'log.message'                      (required when kind=event)
request_fixture  spec fixture path (relative to bridge/spec/fixtures) (required when kind=request)
its /params object is sent as the request params
expect_fixture   spec fixture path (relative to bridge/spec/fixtures) (required)
request: its /result is the expected value
event:   its /params is the expected value
ignore           list of JSON Pointers, relative to the compared value root,
removed from BOTH the actual and expected value before comparison.
Every field NOT listed in ignore is compared with strict value-equality;
any drift there fails the conformance run. That strictness is the point:
a protocol change to a non-ignored field is always detected.
Per-step ignore rationale (actual mock response vs spec positive fixture):
bridge.hello         /sessionId   mock returns 'sess-mock-1', fixture pins 'sess-7f3a' (non-deterministic id).
/capabilities mock HelloResult carries no capabilities field; fixture lists two.
After masking both, the remaining {protocolVersion, server} match exactly.
bridge.getCapabilities none - the mock getCapabilities was aligned to the spec positive fixture, so
the full result (the six capabilityDescriptors runtime.control, log.stream,
viewport.focus, scene.query, object.query, object.edit) is strict-compared. Any drift on this method is detected.
engine.getStatus     none - mock StatusSnapshot equals the fixture result field-for-field.
runtime.play         none - mock PlayAck {accepted:true,requestedState:'playing'} equals the fixture.
log.message          /timestamp mock LogMessageEvent leaves timestamp unset (omitted); the fixture pins
a wall-clock value. After masking, {level, category, message} match exactly.
scene.getTree        none - mock returns the static demo tree (Root -> NodeA / GroupNode -> NodeB)
value-equal to the fixture result. Any drift on this method is detected.
object.getSnapshot   none - mock returns the n-1 property bag value-equal to the fixture result.
This step runs BEFORE object.setProperty so fieldOfView is still its initial 60.
schema.getSnapshot   none - mock returns the type descriptors (TypeA / TypeB) value-equal to the fixture.
object.setProperty   none - mock echoes params.value as appliedValue and updates its in-memory map.
The request fixture sends {property:'fieldOfView', value:75}; the mock returns
{accepted:true, appliedValue:75}, value-equal to the fixture result. This step is
intentionally LAST so its in-memory mutation does not affect the getSnapshot step.
